campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 5, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to hear from the Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority about their investments and spending for March 2026. The council also voted on a new policy that prevents the city from letting federal agencies use city property, and discussed efforts to hire and keep city employees.
keyTopics: Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority's March 2026 financial report
New policy restricting federal agency use of city property
City recruitment and employee retention efforts

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — April 15, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a hearing about cleaning up hazardous materials on certain properties that the County identified as problems. They voted to approve a plan to remove these hazards and also adopted a new technology policy to replace the old one. The Council also set up a formal process for residents to request accommodations if they need them.
keyTopics: Public hearing on removing hazardous materials from local properties
Council approved hazard abatement orders for County-identified sites
New Technology Disruption Policy adopted to update city rules
New process established for residents to request reasonable accommodations

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — May 5, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on May 5, 2026, to approve meeting minutes and address several infrastructure and maintenance issues. The council voted on declaring overgrown brush and hazardous vegetation as public nuisances, reviewed a landscape and lighting maintenance report, and approved multi-year service agreements with outside contractors.
keyTopics: Hazardous brush and overgrown vegetation declared a public nuisance
Landscape and lighting maintenance engineer's report approved
Multi-year service contracts authorized with Pacific (and other vendors)
Previous meeting minutes from April 21, 2026 approved

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 12, 2026
san-jose.summary: San José city leaders met on May 12, 2026, to approve routine paperwork and discuss two money-related initiatives. They updated their banking relationship with Wells Fargo and introduced a new program to help residents who are struggling to pay their water bills.
keyTopics: New financial assistance program for water customers
Updated banking agreement with Wells Fargo
Consulting contract amendment with Kitchell/CEM, Inc.
Approval of previous meeting minutes

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — May 12, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The City Council held a private meeting to discuss two sensitive matters: a potential lawsuit related to a water main problem at Cuesta Park, and negotiations to buy or lease property at two addresses on Clyde Avenue. These closed sessions allow the city to discuss legal and financial matters privately before taking public action.
keyTopics: Cuesta Park water main incident - potential lawsuit
Property acquisition/negotiation at 485 and 495 Clyde Avenue
Legal strategy discussion with city attorney
Real estate negotiation with property owners

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — May 5, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special closed-door meeting at 5 P.M. to discuss confidential matters, followed by a regular public meeting at 7 P.M. The agenda focused on special orders and small business topics, though specific details about what was decided aren't available since the closed session portion wasn't open to the public.
keyTopics: Closed session meeting (private discussion - details not public)
Small business support or initiatives
Special orders from City Council
Regular council meeting convened at 7 P.M.

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 11, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a preliminary meeting to discuss the city's budget plan for July 2026 through June 2027. The council also reviewed rules about lobbyists—people who try to influence city decisions—and what they need to register and report to the city.
keyTopics: FY 2026-27 budget planning and spending priorities
Lobbyist registration and disclosure requirements
Rules for people attempting to influence city decisions

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — May 11, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara's City Council held a meeting on May 11, 2026 focused on Economic Development & Marketing Committee business. The city is offering both in-person attendance and remote participation options via Zoom or phone for residents who want to join or comment.
keyTopics: Economic Development & Marketing Committee (Ad Hoc) meetings
In-person and remote participation options available
Public comment submission via email before 11 AM on meeting day
Multiple ways to join: Zoom webinar, phone call, or in-person attendance

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 5, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to approve their spending plans for the next several years. They discussed a five-year capital improvement plan (money for big projects like roads and buildings), reviewed the upcoming year's operating budget, and approved a new master plan for parks and trails in the Innovation District area.
keyTopics: Approved 5-year capital projects plan (2027-2031)
Reviewed FY 2026-27 operating budget proposal
Adopted Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan
Updated City Council procedures and meeting rules
Approved previous meeting minutes

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — May 7, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The city council held a hearing about a plan to build a 6-story apartment building with 72 units at 450 Lytton Avenue, replacing the city's current surface parking lot (Lot T). All 72 apartments would be permanently affordable for low-income residents, and the developer is asking for special approval to build at higher density than normal zoning allows.
keyTopics: 72 new affordable housing units on Lytton Avenue
6-story building replacing 52-space city parking lot
Request for density bonus and zoning waivers under state law
Environmental review (CEQA exemption being evaluated)
